01_initial_request.input_data: Residual nasopharyngeal samples obtained on days 0, 31, and 44 from an immunocompromised patient with HIV and persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection
01_initial_request.user_intent: Investigate SARS-CoV-2 evolution and escape from combination monoclonal antibody treatment in an immunocompromised individual
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Full-length viral genome sequences and identification of intrasample single-nucleotide variants (iSNVs) and consensus-level mutations associated with antibody escape

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Patient presented with persistent SARS-CoV-2 infection and newly diagnosed HIV with severe immunosuppression (CD4 count 14 cells/μL). [Gap] Need to obtain viral samples at multiple time points to track evolution. [Decision] To achieve longitudinal viral evolution tracking, chose to use nasopharyngeal swab collection to perform sample acquisition on patient, expecting to obtain residual nasopharyngeal samples at days 0, 31, and 44.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Three nasopharyngeal samples obtained with SARS-CoV-2 Ct values of 25.7 (day 0), 15.2 (day 31), and 14.9 (day 44)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Samples collected at three time points from persistent infection. [Gap] Need to prepare viral RNA for sequencing. [Decision] To achieve full-length genome sequencing, chose to use library construction to perform viral library preparation on samples, expecting to obtain sequencing-ready libraries.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Libraries constructed using 2 different approaches for all three time point samples

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Libraries prepared from three time point samples. [Gap] Need to obtain viral genome sequences. [Decision] To achieve full-length viral genome sequences, chose to use short-read sequencing to perform genome sequencing on libraries, expecting to obtain complete SARS-CoV-2 genomes with high depth.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Successfully sequenced complete SARS-CoV-2 genomes with high depth at days 0, 31, and 44

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Complete genomes sequenced at three time points. [Gap] Need to identify within-sample viral variation. [Decision] To achieve iSNV identification, chose to use iSNV analysis method to perform intrasample single-nucleotide variant analysis on sequence data, expecting to obtain iSNV frequencies and positions.

parameters.method: rigorous method for analyzing intrasample single-nucleotide variants

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 78 iSNVs identified across 3 time points with variability across the genome, largest number in spike gene

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] iSNVs identified across time points. [Gap] Need to determine viral lineage and evolutionary relationships. [Decision] To achieve lineage classification, chose to use phylogenetic tree construction to perform sequence clustering on genomes, expecting to obtain lineage assignment and phylogenetic relationships.

parameters.input: complete SARS-CoV-2 genomes from days 0, 31, and 44

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All sequences belonged to Delta lineage AY.119 and clustered together on phylogenetic tree

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phylogenetic analysis completed showing Delta lineage AY.119. [Gap] Need to identify consensus-level mutations and track frequency changes. [Decision] To achieve mutation tracking, chose to use mutation frequency analysis to perform consensus and iSNV frequency tracking on sequence data, expecting to obtain mutation frequencies and haplotype information.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 8 consensus-level mutations arose over infection course; 12 mutations changed frequency by 10% or more; 2 potential haplotypes identified based on allele frequencies

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutations and haplotypes identified including spike gene mutations. [Gap] Need to determine if mutations confer antibody escape. [Decision] To achieve escape mutation identification, chose to use escape mutation mapping to perform mutation-to-antibody escape comparison on spike mutations, expecting to obtain antibody escape associations.

parameters.reference_study: Starr et al. 2021 scanning mutagenesis study

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Spike V445A confers high degree of escape from imdevimab; spike Y453F confers moderate to high degree of escape from casirivimab; both arose between day 0 and day 31 after monoclonal antibody treatment

references.citation: Starr TN, Greaney AJ, Addetia A, et al. Prospective mapping of viral mutations that escape antibodies used to treat COVID-19. Science 2021; 371:850–4.
references.relevance: Experimental study that mapped escape mutations to therapeutic antibodies using scanning mutagenesis, used to identify escape mutations in patient samples

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Full-length viral genome sequencing with iSNV analysis and escape mutation mapping against known antibody escape data

genome_sequences_obtained.interpretation: Successfully sequenced complete SARS-CoV-2 genomes at days 0, 31, and 44

isnvs_identified.interpretation: Total intrasample single-nucleotide variants identified across 3 time points

consensus_mutations.interpretation: Consensus-level mutations arose over course of infection (7/8 nonsynonymous)

escape_mutations.interpretation: V445A (imdevimab escape) and Y453F (casirivimab escape) identified, both arose after monoclonal antibody treatment

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: Full-length viral genome sequences obtained from all three time points, 78 iSNVs and 8 consensus-level mutations identified, and 2 spike mutations (V445A and Y453F) associated with antibody escape from casirivimab/imdevimab treatment were successfully identified and linked to treatment timeline
